claim: Context window = input tokens (system prompt, messages) + output tokens; hitting the limit causes errors or early stopping during generation
actionable: Monitor context usage in coding agents; understand that both input and output count against the limit
source_title: Most devs don't understand how context windows work

claim: All LLMs suffer from lost-in-the-middle problem: information in the middle of long context is deprioritized; start and end of conversation have most impact
actionable: Structure prompts with critical information at start and end; regularly clear context windows to avoid lost-in-the-middle degradation
source_title: Most devs don't understand how context windows work

claim: Larger context windows don't always improve performance; model performance degrades with more information due to retrieval difficulty (needle-in-haystack problem)
actionable: Prefer lean, focused context windows; avoid bloating context with unnecessary information even if the model supports large windows
source_title: Most devs don't understand how context windows work

claim: Regularly running /clear between tasks is better than /compact; clear refreshes memory and avoids lost-in-the-middle; compact preserves conversation history but takes time and tokens
actionable: Use /clear as your default for new tasks; only use /compact if you need to preserve conversation context for reference
source_title: Most devs don't understand how context windows work

claim: MCP servers can rapidly bloat context; be cautious about adding multiple MCPs since tool definitions consume significant tokens
actionable: Use MCP servers sparingly; monitor which MCPs are loaded and their context cost; prefer targeted, minimal setups
source_title: Most devs don't understand how context windows work
